summary: Unified Participant/Organizer View - All tests passed. The new architecture where authenticated participants access /appointments/{id} instead of /invitation/{token} is working correctly. Backend correctly returns viewer_role='participant' with viewer_participant_id and viewer_invitation_token. Frontend correctly hides organizer-only sections (calendar sync, cancel, edit, manage participants, video ingestion) while showing participant-specific elements (action banner, trust signal, engagement summary, financial breakdown, proof section read-only).

backend_pytest: ✅ GET /api/appointments/{id} returns viewer_role='participant' for participant user
✅ GET /api/appointments/{id} returns viewer_role='organizer' for workspace member
✅ Appointment response includes all required fields for participant view
✅ GET /api/appointments/my-timeline returns participant items with appointment_id
✅ Participant timeline items have all required fields
frontend_playwright: ✅ Participant view: engagement summary visible (50 EUR, 15 min tolérance, 0h annulation)
✅ Participant view: financial breakdown visible (70% compensation, 20% commission, 10% charity)
✅ Participant view: trust signal banner visible ('1 participant a déjà confirmé son engagement')
✅ Participant view: action banner visible with Accept/Decline buttons (status='invited')
✅ Participant view: NO 'Gérer les participants' button
✅ Participant view: NO calendar sync section
✅ Participant view: NO resend invitation buttons
✅ Participant view: NO edit pencil button
✅ Organizer view: 'Gérer les participants' button visible
✅ Organizer view: calendar sync buttons visible (.ics, Google)
✅ Dashboard: participant items link to /appointments/{id} NOT /invitation/{token}

critical_code_review_comments: ✅ Backend appointments.py correctly checks workspace membership first, then participant match
✅ Backend returns viewer_role='participant' with viewer_participant_id and viewer_invitation_token
✅ Backend participants.py correctly allows participant access to list participants
✅ Frontend AppointmentDetail.js correctly uses isOrganizer flag to conditionally render sections
✅ Frontend ParticipantsSection.js correctly hides 'Gérer les participants' and resend buttons for participants
✅ Frontend AppointmentHeader.js correctly hides organizer-only actions for participants
✅ Frontend OrganizerDashboard.js correctly links participant items to /appointments/{id}
context_for_next_testing_agent: Unified participant/organizer view verified. Authenticated participants now access /appointments/{id} instead of /invitation/{token}. Backend GET /api/appointments/{id} returns viewer_role='participant' with viewer_participant_id and viewer_invitation_token when user is participant. Frontend conditionally hides organizer-only sections (calendar sync, cancel, edit, manage participants, video ingestion) based on isOrganizer flag. Dashboard participant items correctly link to /appointments/{id}. The /invitation/{token} page is preserved for non-authenticated users.

backend_access_control.description: GET /api/appointments/{id} checks workspace membership first, then participant match
backend_access_control.verification: Returns viewer_role='participant' or 'organizer' based on access type

participant_list_access.verification: Participants can view list but cannot add/modify

frontend_conditional_rendering.description: AppointmentDetail.js uses isOrganizer flag to show/hide sections

dashboard_links.description: Participant items in dashboard link to /appointments/{id}

mocked_apis.Stripe: Dev mode - payment methods are pm_dev_* format
